action.content:
Correctly close a Dyck-n word.

Q: Complete the rest of the sequence, making sure that the parentheses are closed properly. Input: [ { [
A: ] } ]

Q: Complete the rest of the sequence, making sure that the parentheses are closed properly. Input: < > ( ( [ [ ( { } ) [ < > ] ]
A: ] ) )

Q: Complete the rest of the sequence, making sure that the parentheses are closed properly. Input: < [ < [ { < [ ] < { } > > } ] > { { ( ) } { < [ < > ] > }
A: } ] >

Q: Complete the rest of the sequence, making sure that the parentheses are closed properly. Input: < { < ( )
A:
action.label: context

action.content:
To correctly close the sequence, we need to match the opening brackets with their corresponding closing brackets. 

The input is: < { < ( )

We start by counting the number of opening and closing brackets. There are 2 opening brackets: < and (, and 1 closing bracket: ).

To close the sequence properly, we need to close the remaining opening brackets. The first opening bracket is <, so we close it with >. The second opening bracket is (, so we close it with ).

The correct completion of the sequence is: > )
action.label: response
